2442	 * require a lot of memory (because of keeping all the values).
2443	 */ view japanese
2444	if (column->bv_allnulls)
2445	{
2446		MemoryContext oldctx;
2447
2448		int			target_maxvalues;
2449		int			maxvalues;
2450		BlockNumber pagesPerRange = BrinGetPagesPerRange(bdesc->bd_index);
2451
2452		/* what was specified as a reloption? */ view japanese
2453		target_maxvalues = brin_minmax_multi_get_values(bdesc, opts);
2454
2455		/*
2456		 * Determine the insert buffer size - we use 10x the target, capped to
2457		 * the maximum number of values in the heap range. This is more than
2458		 * enough, considering the actual number of rows per page is likely
2459		 * much lower, but meh.
2460		 */
2461		maxvalues = Min(target_maxvalues * MINMAX_BUFFER_FACTOR,
2462						MaxHeapTuplesPerPage * pagesPerRange);
2463
2464		/* but always at least the original value */
2465		maxvalues = Max(maxvalues, target_maxvalues);
2466
2467		/* always cap by MIN/MAX */
2468		maxvalues = Max(maxvalues, MINMAX_BUFFER_MIN);
2469		maxvalues = Min(maxvalues, MINMAX_BUFFER_MAX);
2470
2471		oldctx = MemoryContextSwitchTo(column->bv_context);
2472		ranges = minmax_multi_init(maxvalues);
2473		ranges->attno = attno;
2474		ranges->colloid = colloid;
2475		ranges->typid = attr->atttypid;
2476		ranges->target_maxvalues = target_maxvalues;
2477
2478		/* we'll certainly need the comparator, so just look it up now */
2479		ranges->cmp = minmax_multi_get_strategy_procinfo(bdesc, attno, attr->atttypid,
2480														 BTLessStrategyNumber);
2481
2482		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldctx);
2483
2484		column->bv_allnulls = false;
2485		modified = true;
2486
2487		column->bv_mem_value = PointerGetDatum(ranges);
2488		column->bv_serialize = brin_minmax_multi_serialize;
2489	}
2490	else if (!ranges)
2491	{
2492		MemoryContext oldctx;
2493
2494		int			maxvalues;
2495		BlockNumber pagesPerRange = BrinGetPagesPerRange(bdesc->bd_index);
2496
2497		oldctx = MemoryContextSwitchTo(column->bv_context);
2498
2499		serialized = (SerializedRanges *) PG_DETOAST_DATUM(column->bv_values[0]);
2500
2501		/*
2502		 * Determine the insert buffer size - we use 10x the target, capped to
2503		 * the maximum number of values in the heap range. This is more than
2504		 * enough, considering the actual number of rows per page is likely
2505		 * much lower, but meh.
2506		 */ view japanese
2507		maxvalues = Min(serialized->maxvalues * MINMAX_BUFFER_FACTOR,
2508						MaxHeapTuplesPerPage * pagesPerRange);
2509
2510		/* but always at least the original value */ view japanese
2511		maxvalues = Max(maxvalues, serialized->maxvalues);
2512
2513		/* always cap by MIN/MAX */ view japanese
2514		maxvalues = Max(maxvalues, MINMAX_BUFFER_MIN);
2515		maxvalues = Min(maxvalues, MINMAX_BUFFER_MAX);
2516
2517		ranges = brin_range_deserialize(maxvalues, serialized);
2518
2519		ranges->attno = attno;
2520		ranges->colloid = colloid;
2521		ranges->typid = attr->atttypid;
2522
2523		/* we'll certainly need the comparator, so just look it up now */ view japanese
2524		ranges->cmp = minmax_multi_get_strategy_procinfo(bdesc, attno, attr->atttypid,
2525														 BTLessStrategyNumber);
2526
2527		column->bv_mem_value = PointerGetDatum(ranges);
2528		column->bv_serialize = brin_minmax_multi_serialize;
2529
2530		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldctx);
2531	}
2532
2533	/*
2534	 * Try to add the new value to the range. We need to update the modified
2535	 * flag, so that we serialize the updated summary later.
2536	 */ view japanese
2537	modified |= range_add_value(bdesc, colloid, attno, attr, ranges, newval);
2538
2539
2540	PG_RETURN_BOOL(modified);
2541}
2542
2543/*
2544 * Given an index tuple corresponding to a certain page range and a scan key,
2545 * return whether the scan key is consistent with the index tuple's min/max
2546 * values.  Return true if so, false otherwise.
2547 */ view japanese
2548Datum
2549brin_minmax_multi_consistent(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2550{
2551	BrinDesc   *bdesc = (BrinDesc *) PG_GETARG_POINTER(0);
2552	BrinValues *column = (BrinValues *) PG_GETARG_POINTER(1);
2553	ScanKey    *keys = (ScanKey *) PG_GETARG_POINTER(2);
2554	int			nkeys = PG_GETARG_INT32(3);
2555
2556	Oid			colloid = PG_GET_COLLATION(),
2557				subtype;
2558	AttrNumber	attno;
2559	Datum		value;
2560	FmgrInfo   *finfo;
2561	SerializedRanges *serialized;
2562	Ranges	   *ranges;
2563	int			keyno;
2564	int			rangeno;
2565	int			i;
2566
2567	attno = column->bv_attno;
2568
2569	serialized = (SerializedRanges *) PG_DETOAST_DATUM(column->bv_values[0]);
2570	ranges = brin_range_deserialize(serialized->maxvalues, serialized);
2571
2572	/* inspect the ranges, and for each one evaluate the scan keys */ view japanese
2573	for (rangeno = 0; rangeno < ranges->nranges; rangeno++)
2574	{
2575		Datum		minval = ranges->values[2 * rangeno];
2576		Datum		maxval = ranges->values[2 * rangeno + 1];
2577
2578		/* assume the range is matching, and we'll try to prove otherwise */
2579		bool		matching = true;
2580
2581		for (keyno = 0; keyno < nkeys; keyno++)
2582		{
2583			bool		matches;
2584			ScanKey		key = keys[keyno];
2585
2586			/* NULL keys are handled and filtered-out in bringetbitmap */ view japanese
2587			Assert(!(key->sk_flags & SK_ISNULL));
2588
2589			attno = key->sk_attno;
2590			subtype = key->sk_subtype;
2591			value = key->sk_argument;
2592			switch (key->sk_strategy)
2593			{
2594				case BTLessStrategyNumber:
2595				case BTLessEqualStrategyNumber:
2596					finfo = minmax_multi_get_strategy_procinfo(bdesc, attno, subtype,
2597															   key->sk_strategy);
2598					/* first value from the array */ view japanese
2599					matches = DatumGetBool(FunctionCall2Coll(finfo, colloid, minval, value));
2600					break;
2601
2602				case BTEqualStrategyNumber:
2603					{
2604						Datum		compar;
2605						FmgrInfo   *cmpFn;
2606
2607						/* by default this range does not match */ view japanese
2608						matches = false;
2609
2610						/*
2611						 * Otherwise, need to compare the new value with
2612						 * boundaries of all the ranges. First check if it's
2613						 * less than the absolute minimum, which is the first
2614						 * value in the array.
2615						 */ view japanese
2616						cmpFn = minmax_multi_get_strategy_procinfo(bdesc, attno, subtype,
2617																   BTGreaterStrategyNumber);
2618						compar = FunctionCall2Coll(cmpFn, colloid, minval, value);
2619
2620						/* smaller than the smallest value in this range */ view japanese
2621						if (DatumGetBool(compar))
2622							break;
2623
2624						cmpFn = minmax_multi_get_strategy_procinfo(bdesc, attno, subtype,
2625																   BTLessStrategyNumber);
2626						compar = FunctionCall2Coll(cmpFn, colloid, maxval, value);
2627
2628						/* larger than the largest value in this range */ view japanese
2629						if (DatumGetBool(compar))
2630							break;
2631
2632						/*
2633						 * We haven't managed to eliminate this range, so
2634						 * consider it matching.
2635						 */ view japanese
2636						matches = true;
2637
2638						break;
2639					}
2640				case BTGreaterEqualStrategyNumber:
2641				case BTGreaterStrategyNumber:
2642					finfo = minmax_multi_get_strategy_procinfo(bdesc, attno, subtype,
2643															   key->sk_strategy);
2644					/* last value from the array */ view japanese
2645					matches = DatumGetBool(FunctionCall2Coll(finfo, colloid, maxval, value));
2646					break;
2647
2648				default:
2649					/* shouldn't happen */
2650					elog(ERROR, "invalid strategy number %d", key->sk_strategy);
2651					matches = false;
2652					break;
2653			}
2654
2655			/* the range has to match all the scan keys */
2656			matching &= matches;
2657
2658			/* once we find a non-matching key, we're done */
2659			if (!matching)
2660				break;
2661		}
2662
2663		/*
2664		 * have we found a range matching all scan keys? if yes, we're done
2665		 */ view japanese
2666		if (matching)
2667			PG_RETURN_BOOL(true);
2668	}
2669
2670	/*
2671	 * And now inspect the values. We don't bother with doing a binary search
2672	 * here, because we're dealing with serialized / fully compacted ranges,
2673	 * so there should be only very few values.
2674	 */ view japanese
2675	for (i = 0; i < ranges->nvalues; i++)
2676	{
2677		Datum		val = ranges->values[2 * ranges->nranges + i];
2678
2679		/* assume the range is matching, and we'll try to prove otherwise */ view japanese
2680		bool		matching = true;
2681
2682		for (keyno = 0; keyno < nkeys; keyno++)
2683		{
2684			bool		matches;
2685			ScanKey		key = keys[keyno];
2686
2687			/* we've already dealt with NULL keys at the beginning */ view japanese
2688			if (key->sk_flags & SK_ISNULL)
2689				continue;
2690
2691			attno = key->sk_attno;
2692			subtype = key->sk_subtype;
2693			value = key->sk_argument;
2694			switch (key->sk_strategy)
2695			{
2696				case BTLessStrategyNumber:
2697				case BTLessEqualStrategyNumber:
2698				case BTEqualStrategyNumber:
2699				case BTGreaterEqualStrategyNumber:
2700				case BTGreaterStrategyNumber:
2701
2702					finfo = minmax_multi_get_strategy_procinfo(bdesc, attno, subtype,
2703															   key->sk_strategy);
2704					matches = DatumGetBool(FunctionCall2Coll(finfo, colloid, val, value));
2705					break;
2706
2707				default:
2708					/* shouldn't happen */ view japanese
2709					elog(ERROR, "invalid strategy number %d", key->sk_strategy);
2710					matches = false;
2711					break;
2712			}
2713
2714			/* the range has to match all the scan keys */ view japanese
2715			matching &= matches;
2716
2717			/* once we find a non-matching key, we're done */ view japanese
2718			if (!matching)
2719				break;
2720		}
2721
2722		/* have we found a range matching all scan keys? if yes, we're done */ view japanese
2723		if (matching)
2724			PG_RETURN_BOOL(true);
2725	}
2726
2727	PG_RETURN_BOOL(false);
2728}
2729
2730/*
2731 * Given two BrinValues, update the first of them as a union of the summary
2732 * values contained in both.  The second one is untouched.
2733 */ view japanese
2734Datum
2735brin_minmax_multi_union(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2736{
2737	BrinDesc   *bdesc = (BrinDesc *) PG_GETARG_POINTER(0);
2738	BrinValues *col_a = (BrinValues *) PG_GETARG_POINTER(1);
2739	BrinValues *col_b = (BrinValues *) PG_GETARG_POINTER(2);
2740
2741	Oid			colloid = PG_GET_COLLATION();
2742	SerializedRanges *serialized_a;
2743	SerializedRanges *serialized_b;
2744	Ranges	   *ranges_a;
2745	Ranges	   *ranges_b;
2746	AttrNumber	attno;
2747	Form_pg_attribute attr;
2748	ExpandedRange *eranges;
2749	int			neranges;
2750	FmgrInfo   *cmpFn,
2751			   *distanceFn;
2752	DistanceValue *distances;
2753	MemoryContext ctx;
2754	MemoryContext oldctx;
2755
2756	Assert(col_a->bv_attno == col_b->bv_attno);
2757	Assert(!col_a->bv_allnulls && !col_b->bv_allnulls);
2758
2759	attno = col_a->bv_attno;
2760	attr = TupleDescAttr(bdesc->bd_tupdesc, attno - 1);
2761
2762	serialized_a = (SerializedRanges *) PG_DETOAST_DATUM(col_a->bv_values[0]);
2763	serialized_b = (SerializedRanges *) PG_DETOAST_DATUM(col_b->bv_values[0]);
2764
2765	ranges_a = brin_range_deserialize(serialized_a->maxvalues, serialized_a);
2766	ranges_b = brin_range_deserialize(serialized_b->maxvalues, serialized_b);
2767
2768	/* make sure neither of the ranges is NULL */ view japanese
2769	Assert(ranges_a && ranges_b);
2770
2771	neranges = (ranges_a->nranges + ranges_a->nvalues) +
2772		(ranges_b->nranges + ranges_b->nvalues);
2773
2774	/*
2775	 * The distanceFn calls (which may internally call e.g. numeric_le) may
2776	 * allocate quite a bit of memory, and we must not leak it. Otherwise,
2777	 * we'd have problems e.g. when building indexes. So we create a local
2778	 * memory context and make sure we free the memory before leaving this
2779	 * function (not after every call).
2780	 */ view japanese
2781	ctx = AllocSetContextCreate(CurrentMemoryContext,
2782								"minmax-multi context",
2783								ALLOCSET_DEFAULT_SIZES);
2784
2785	oldctx = MemoryContextSwitchTo(ctx);
2786
2787	/* allocate and fill */ view japanese
2788	eranges = (ExpandedRange *) palloc0(neranges * sizeof(ExpandedRange));
2789
2790	/* fill the expanded ranges with entries for the first range */ view japanese
2791	fill_expanded_ranges(eranges, ranges_a->nranges + ranges_a->nvalues,
2792						 ranges_a);
2793
2794	/* and now add combine ranges for the second range */ view japanese
2795	fill_expanded_ranges(&eranges[ranges_a->nranges + ranges_a->nvalues],
2796						 ranges_b->nranges + ranges_b->nvalues,
2797						 ranges_b);
2798
2799	cmpFn = minmax_multi_get_strategy_procinfo(bdesc, attno, attr->atttypid,
2800											   BTLessStrategyNumber);
2801
2802	/* sort the expanded ranges */ view japanese
2803	neranges = sort_expanded_ranges(cmpFn, colloid, eranges, neranges);
2804
2805	/*
2806	 * We've loaded two different lists of expanded ranges, so some of them
2807	 * may be overlapping. So walk through them and merge them.
2808	 */ view japanese
2809	neranges = merge_overlapping_ranges(cmpFn, colloid, eranges, neranges);
2810
2811	/* check that the combine ranges are correct (no overlaps, ordering) */ view japanese
2812	AssertCheckExpandedRanges(bdesc, colloid, attno, attr, eranges, neranges);
2813
2814	/*
2815	 * If needed, reduce some of the ranges.
2816	 *
2817	 * XXX This may be fairly expensive, so maybe we should do it only when
2818	 * it's actually needed (when we have too many ranges).
2819	 */ view japanese
2820
2821	/* build array of gap distances and sort them in ascending order */ view japanese
2822	distanceFn = minmax_multi_get_procinfo(bdesc, attno, PROCNUM_DISTANCE);
2823	distances = build_distances(distanceFn, colloid, eranges, neranges);
2824
2825	/*
2826	 * See how many values would be needed to store the current ranges, and if
2827	 * needed combine as many of them to get below the threshold. The
2828	 * collapsed ranges will be stored as a single value.
2829	 *
2830	 * XXX This does not apply the load factor, as we don't expect to add more
2831	 * values to the range, so we prefer to keep as many ranges as possible.
2832	 *
2833	 * XXX Can the maxvalues be different in the two ranges? Perhaps we should
2834	 * use maximum of those?
2835	 */ view japanese
2836	neranges = reduce_expanded_ranges(eranges, neranges, distances,
2837									  ranges_a->maxvalues,
2838									  cmpFn, colloid);
2839
2840	/* Is the result of reducing expanded ranges correct? */ view japanese
2841	AssertCheckExpandedRanges(bdesc, colloid, attno, attr, eranges, neranges);
2842
2843	/* update the first range summary */ view japanese
2844	store_expanded_ranges(ranges_a, eranges, neranges);
2845
2846	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldctx);
2847	MemoryContextDelete(ctx);
2848
2849	/* cleanup and update the serialized value */ view japanese
2850	pfree(serialized_a);
2851	col_a->bv_values[0] = PointerGetDatum(brin_range_serialize(ranges_a));
2852
2853	PG_RETURN_VOID();
2854}
2855
2856/*
2857 * Cache and return minmax multi opclass support procedure
2858 *
2859 * Return the procedure corresponding to the given function support number
2860 * or null if it does not exist.
2861 */ view japanese
2862static FmgrInfo *
2863minmax_multi_get_procinfo(BrinDesc *bdesc, uint16 attno, uint16 procnum)
2864{
2865	MinmaxMultiOpaque *opaque;
2866	uint16		basenum = procnum - PROCNUM_BASE;
2867
2868	/*
2869	 * We cache these in the opaque struct, to avoid repetitive syscache
2870	 * lookups.
2871	 */ view japanese
2872	opaque = (MinmaxMultiOpaque *) bdesc->bd_info[attno - 1]->oi_opaque;
2873
2874	if (opaque->extra_procinfos[basenum].fn_oid == InvalidOid)
2875	{
2876		if (RegProcedureIsValid(index_getprocid(bdesc->bd_index, attno,
2877												procnum)))
2878			fmgr_info_copy(&opaque->extra_procinfos[basenum],
2879						   index_getprocinfo(bdesc->bd_index, attno, procnum),
2880						   bdesc->bd_context);
2881		else
2882			ereport(ERROR,
2883					errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2884					errmsg_internal("invalid opclass definition"),
2885					errdetail_internal("The operator class is missing support function %d for column %d.",
2886									   procnum, attno));
2887	}
2888
2889	return &opaque->extra_procinfos[basenum];
2890}
2891
2892/*
2893 * Cache and return the procedure for the given strategy.
2894 *
2895 * Note: this function mirrors minmax_multi_get_strategy_procinfo; see notes
2896 * there.  If changes are made here, see that function too.
2897 */ view japanese
2898static FmgrInfo *
2899minmax_multi_get_strategy_procinfo(BrinDesc *bdesc, uint16 attno, Oid subtype,
2900								   uint16 strategynum)
2901{
2902	MinmaxMultiOpaque *opaque;
2903
2904	Assert(strategynum >= 1 &&
2905		   strategynum <= BTMaxStrategyNumber);
2906
2907	opaque = (MinmaxMultiOpaque *) bdesc->bd_info[attno - 1]->oi_opaque;
2908
2909	/*
2910	 * We cache the procedures for the previous subtype in the opaque struct,
2911	 * to avoid repetitive syscache lookups.  If the subtype changed,
2912	 * invalidate all the cached entries.
2913	 */ view japanese
2914	if (opaque->cached_subtype != subtype)
2915	{
2916		uint16		i;
2917
2918		for (i = 1; i <= BTMaxStrategyNumber; i++)
2919			opaque->strategy_procinfos[i - 1].fn_oid = InvalidOid;
2920		opaque->cached_subtype = subtype;
2921	}
2922
2923	if (opaque->strategy_procinfos[strategynum - 1].fn_oid == InvalidOid)
2924	{
2925		Form_pg_attribute attr;
2926		HeapTuple	tuple;
2927		Oid			opfamily,
2928					oprid;
2929
2930		opfamily = bdesc->bd_index->rd_opfamily[attno - 1];
2931		attr = TupleDescAttr(bdesc->bd_tupdesc, attno - 1);
2932		tuple = SearchSysCache4(AMOPSTRATEGY, ObjectIdGetDatum(opfamily),
2933								ObjectIdGetDatum(attr->atttypid),
2934								ObjectIdGetDatum(subtype),
2935								Int16GetDatum(strategynum));
2936		if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
2937			elog(ERROR, "missing operator %d(%u,%u) in opfamily %u",
2938				 strategynum, attr->atttypid, subtype, opfamily);
2939
2940		oprid = DatumGetObjectId(SysCacheGetAttrNotNull(AMOPSTRATEGY, tuple,
2941														Anum_pg_amop_amopopr));
2942		ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
2943		Assert(RegProcedureIsValid(oprid));
2944
2945		fmgr_info_cxt(get_opcode(oprid),
2946					  &opaque->strategy_procinfos[strategynum - 1],
2947					  bdesc->bd_context);
2948	}
2949
2950	return &opaque->strategy_procinfos[strategynum - 1];
2951}
2952
2953Datum
2954brin_minmax_multi_options(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2955{
2956	local_relopts *relopts = (local_relopts *) PG_GETARG_POINTER(0);
2957
2958	init_local_reloptions(relopts, sizeof(MinMaxMultiOptions));
2959
2960	add_local_int_reloption(relopts, "values_per_range", "desc",
2961							MINMAX_MULTI_DEFAULT_VALUES_PER_PAGE, 8, 256,
2962							offsetof(MinMaxMultiOptions, valuesPerRange));
2963
2964	PG_RETURN_VOID();
2965}
2966
2967/*
2968 * brin_minmax_multi_summary_in
2969 *		- input routine for type brin_minmax_multi_summary.
2970 *
2971 * brin_minmax_multi_summary is only used internally to represent summaries
2972 * in BRIN minmax-multi indexes, so it has no operations of its own, and we
2973 * disallow input too.
2974 */ view japanese
2975Datum
2976brin_minmax_multi_summary_in(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2977{
2978	/*
2979	 * brin_minmax_multi_summary stores the data in binary form and parsing
2980	 * text input is not needed, so disallow this.
2981	 */ view japanese
2982	ereport(ERROR,
2983			(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
2984			 errmsg("cannot accept a value of type %s", "brin_minmax_multi_summary")));
2985
2986	PG_RETURN_VOID();			/* keep compiler quiet */
2987}
2988
2989
2990/*
2991 * brin_minmax_multi_summary_out
2992 *		- output routine for type brin_minmax_multi_summary.
2993 *
2994 * BRIN minmax-multi summaries are serialized into a bytea value, but we
2995 * want to output something nicer humans can understand.
2996 */ view japanese
2997Datum
2998brin_minmax_multi_summary_out(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2999{
3000	int			i;
3001	int			idx;
3002	SerializedRanges *ranges;
3003	Ranges	   *ranges_deserialized;
3004	StringInfoData str;
3005	bool		isvarlena;
3006	Oid			outfunc;
3007	FmgrInfo	fmgrinfo;
3008	ArrayBuildState *astate_values = NULL;
3009
3010	initStringInfo(&str);
3011	appendStringInfoChar(&str, '{');
3012
3013	/*
3014	 * Detoast to get value with full 4B header (can't be stored in a toast
3015	 * table, but can use 1B header).
3016	 */ view japanese
3017	ranges = (SerializedRanges *) PG_DETOAST_DATUM(PG_GETARG_DATUM(0));
3018
3019	/* lookup output func for the type */ view japanese
3020	getTypeOutputInfo(ranges->typid, &outfunc, &isvarlena);
3021	fmgr_info(outfunc, &fmgrinfo);
3022
3023	/* deserialize the range info easy-to-process pieces */ view japanese
3024	ranges_deserialized = brin_range_deserialize(ranges->maxvalues, ranges);
3025
3026	appendStringInfo(&str, "nranges: %d  nvalues: %d  maxvalues: %d",
3027					 ranges_deserialized->nranges,
3028					 ranges_deserialized->nvalues,
3029					 ranges_deserialized->maxvalues);
3030
3031	/* serialize ranges */ view japanese
3032	idx = 0;
3033	for (i = 0; i < ranges_deserialized->nranges; i++)
3034	{
3035		char	   *a,
3036				   *b;
3037		text	   *c;
3038		StringInfoData buf;
3039
3040		initStringInfo(&buf);
3041
3042		a = OutputFunctionCall(&fmgrinfo, ranges_deserialized->values[idx++]);
3043		b = OutputFunctionCall(&fmgrinfo, ranges_deserialized->values[idx++]);
3044
3045		appendStringInfo(&buf, "%s ... %s", a, b);
3046
3047		c = cstring_to_text_with_len(buf.data, buf.len);
3048
3049		astate_values = accumArrayResult(astate_values,
3050										 PointerGetDatum(c),
3051										 false,
3052										 TEXTOID,
3053										 CurrentMemoryContext);
3054	}
3055
3056	if (ranges_deserialized->nranges > 0)
3057	{
3058		Oid			typoutput;
3059		bool		typIsVarlena;
3060		Datum		val;
3061		char	   *extval;
3062
3063		getTypeOutputInfo(ANYARRAYOID, &typoutput, &typIsVarlena);
3064
3065		val = makeArrayResult(astate_values, CurrentMemoryContext);
3066
3067		extval = OidOutputFunctionCall(typoutput, val);
3068
3069		appendStringInfo(&str, " ranges: %s", extval);
3070	}
3071
3072	/* serialize individual values */ view japanese
3073	astate_values = NULL;
3074
3075	for (i = 0; i < ranges_deserialized->nvalues; i++)
3076	{
3077		Datum		a;
3078		text	   *b;
3079
3080		a = FunctionCall1(&fmgrinfo, ranges_deserialized->values[idx++]);
3081		b = cstring_to_text(DatumGetCString(a));
3082
3083		astate_values = accumArrayResult(astate_values,
3084										 PointerGetDatum(b),
3085										 false,
3086										 TEXTOID,
3087										 CurrentMemoryContext);
3088	}
3089
3090	if (ranges_deserialized->nvalues > 0)
3091	{
3092		Oid			typoutput;
3093		bool		typIsVarlena;
3094		Datum		val;
3095		char	   *extval;
3096
3097		getTypeOutputInfo(ANYARRAYOID, &typoutput, &typIsVarlena);
3098
3099		val = makeArrayResult(astate_values, CurrentMemoryContext);
3100
3101		extval = OidOutputFunctionCall(typoutput, val);
3102
3103		appendStringInfo(&str, " values: %s", extval);
3104	}
3105
3106
3107	appendStringInfoChar(&str, '}');
3108
3109	PG_RETURN_CSTRING(str.data);
3110}
3111
3112/*
3113 * brin_minmax_multi_summary_recv
3114 *		- binary input routine for type brin_minmax_multi_summary.
3115 */ view japanese
3116Datum
3117brin_minmax_multi_summary_recv(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3118{
3119	ereport(ERROR,
3120			(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3121			 errmsg("cannot accept a value of type %s", "brin_minmax_multi_summary")));
3122
3123	PG_RETURN_VOID();			/* keep compiler quiet */ view japanese
3124}
3125
3126/*
3127 * brin_minmax_multi_summary_send
3128 *		- binary output routine for type brin_minmax_multi_summary.
3129 *
3130 * BRIN minmax-multi summaries are serialized in a bytea value (although
3131 * the type is named differently), so let's just send that.
3132 */ view japanese
3133Datum
3134brin_minmax_multi_summary_send(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3135{
3136	return byteasend(fcinfo);
3137}

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:59

/* PGSQL_AF_INET に必要 */

Original comment

/* needed for PGSQL_AF_INET */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:86-91

/*
 * 追加のSQLレベルサポート関数
 *
 * プロシージャ番号には、BRIN自身のために予約されている値を使用してはなりません。
 * brin_internal.h を参照してください。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Additional SQL level support functions
 *
 * Procedure numbers must not use values reserved for BRIN itself; see
 * brin_internal.h.
 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:92

Original comment

/* maximum support procs we need */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:93

/* 必須、値間の距離 */

Original comment

/* required, distance between values */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:95-98

/*
 * MinmaxMultiOpaque配列内のインデックスを取得するために、procnumからこれを減算する
 * （プライベートprocnumの最小値と等しくなければならない）。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Subtract this from procnum to obtain index in MinmaxMultiOpaque arrays
 * (Must be equal to minimum of private procnums).
 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:101-105

/*
 * インサートバッファのサイズ設定 - reloption で指定された値の 10 倍を使用しますが、
 * 大きくなりすぎないよう 8192 を上限とします。バッファが一杯になると、
 * 値の数を半分に減らします。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Sizing the insert buffer - we use 10x the number of values specified
 * in the reloption, but we cap it to 8192 not to get too large. When
 * the buffer gets full, we reduce the number of values by half.
 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:118-120

/*
 * BRINのminmax reloptionsのストレージタイプ
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Storage type for BRIN's minmax reloptions
 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:207

/* varlena ヘッダー (直接触らないこと！) */

Original comment

/* varlena header (do not touch directly!) */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:124

/* 範囲ごとの値の数 */

Original comment

/* number of values per range */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:136-168

/*
 * minmax-multi インデックスのサマリーには、処理に便利な「Ranges」と、
 * bytea 値として格納するための「SerializedRanges」という2つの表現があります。
 *
 * Ranges 構造体は境界値を単一の配列に格納しますが、スペースを節約するために、
 * 通常の範囲と単一点範囲を区別して扱います。（異なる境界値を持つ）通常の範囲では、
 * 範囲の下限と上限の両方を格納する必要がありますが、「単一点範囲」では単一の値を
 * 格納するだけで済みます。
 *
 * 'values' 配列には、まず通常の範囲の境界値（2*nranges 個の値）が格納され、
 * その後に単一点範囲の境界値（nvalues 個）が格納されます。つまり、配列内には
 * 合計で (2*nranges + nvalues) 個の境界値が存在します。
 *
 * +-------------------------+----------------------------------+
 * | ranges (2 * nranges 個) | single point values (nvalues 個) |
 * +-------------------------+----------------------------------+
 *
 * これにより、新しい値を素早く追加したり、既存の範囲値を広げることなく
 * 外れ値を格納したりすることが可能になります。
 *
 * 'nsorted' は、values[] 配列内の 'nvalues' のうち、いくつがソート済みであるかを示します。
 * nsorted == nvalues の場合、すべての単一点値がソート済みであることを意味します。
 *
 * maxvalues（values_per_range reloption で設定）を超える値を格納することはありません。
 * 必要に応じて、範囲の一部をマージします。
 *
 * palloc のオーバーヘッドを最小限に抑えるため、常に maxvalues 個の要素分のスペースを持つ
 * 完全な配列を割り当てます。maxvalues が十分に小さい（64 程度であれば問題ない）限り、
 * これは問題ありません。values_per_range reloption が 256 に制限されているため、
 * この条件は満たされています。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * The summary of minmax-multi indexes has two representations - Ranges for
 * convenient processing, and SerializedRanges for storage in bytea value.
 *
 * The Ranges struct stores the boundary values in a single array, but we
 * treat regular and single-point ranges differently to save space. For
 * regular ranges (with different boundary values) we have to store both
 * the lower and upper bound of the range, while for "single-point ranges"
 * we only need to store a single value.
 *
 * The 'values' array stores boundary values for regular ranges first (there
 * are 2*nranges values to store), and then the nvalues boundary values for
 * single-point ranges. That is, we have (2*nranges + nvalues) boundary
 * values in the array.
 *
 * +-------------------------+----------------------------------+
 * | ranges (2 * nranges of) | single point values (nvalues of) |
 * +-------------------------+----------------------------------+
 *
 * This allows us to quickly add new values, and store outliers without
 * having to widen any of the existing range values.
 *
 * 'nsorted' denotes how many of 'nvalues' in the values[] array are sorted.
 * When nsorted == nvalues, all single point values are sorted.
 *
 * We never store more than maxvalues values (as set by values_per_range
 * reloption). If needed we merge some of the ranges.
 *
 * To minimize palloc overhead, we always allocate the full array with
 * space for maxvalues elements. This should be fine as long as the
 * maxvalues is reasonably small (64 seems fine), which is the case
 * thanks to values_per_range reloption being limited to 256.
 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:171

/* 頻繁に必要となるキャッシュ情報。 */

Original comment

/* Cache information that we need quite often. */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:213

/* (2*nranges + nvalues) <= maxvalues */

Original comment

/* (2*nranges + nvalues) <= maxvalues */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:178

/* values[] 配列内の範囲の数 */

Original comment

/* number of ranges in the values[] array */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:179

/* ソートされたnvaluesの数 */

Original comment

/* number of nvalues which are sorted */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:180

/* values[] 配列内のポイント値の数 */

Original comment

/* number of point values in values[] array */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:181

/* values[] 配列内の要素数 */

Original comment

/* number of elements in the values[] array */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:183-190

/*
	 * 高コストなステップ（ソート、重複排除など）を行わず、単に値を大きなバッファに追加します。
	 * バッファはターゲットとなる値の数の倍数であるため、コンパクションの発生頻度が抑えられ、
	 * コストが償却されます。実際のターゲットは保持しておき、ディスク上の表現にシリアライズする直前の
	 * 最終段階で、要求された数の値にコンパクションを行います。
	 */

Original comment

/*
	 * We simply add the values into a large buffer, without any expensive
	 * steps (sorting, deduplication, ...). The buffer is a multiple of the
	 * target number of values, so the compaction happens less often,
	 * amortizing the costs. We keep the actual target and compact to the
	 * requested number of values at the very end, before serializing to
	 * on-disk representation.
	 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:191

/* 要求された値の数 */

Original comment

/* requested number of values */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:194

/* この範囲に対して格納された値 - 生の値、または範囲のいずれか */

Original comment

/* values stored for this range - either raw values, or ranges */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:198-204

/*
 * オンディスクでは、サマリーは基本的なメタデータを持つ単純なヘッダーと、それに続く境界値からなるbytea値として格納されます。これはvarlenaヘッダーを持つため、直接varlenaとして扱うことができます。
 *
 * シリアライズの詳細については、brin_range_serialize/brin_range_deserializeを参照してください。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * On-disk the summary is stored as a bytea value, with a simple header
 * with basic metadata, followed by the boundary values. It has a varlena
 * header, so can be treated as varlena directly.
 *
 * See brin_range_serialize/brin_range_deserialize for serialization details.
 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:210

/* データ配列に格納される値の型 */

Original comment

/* type of values stored in the data array */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:214

/* 配列内の範囲の数（格納済み） */

Original comment

/* number of ranges in the array (stored) */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:215

/* データ配列内の値の数（すべて） */

Original comment

/* number of values in the data array (all) */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:216

/* 値の最大数 (reloption) */

Original comment

/* maximum number of values (reloption) */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:218

/* 実際のデータが含まれています */

Original comment

/* contains the actual data */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:228-236

/*
 * マージや結合を容易にするために展開された範囲を表すために使用されます。
 *
 * 各展開された範囲は本質的に区間であり、min/max値で表されます。また、それが縮退した範囲（その場合、min値とmax値は等しくなります）であるかどうかを示すフラグも持ちます。このフラグは参照渡し（by-ref）のデータ型を扱うために存在します。データム（datum）を単純に比較することができないため、このフラグによって型固有の比較関数を呼び出す回数を削減できます。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Used to represent ranges expanded to make merging and combining easier.
 *
 * Each expanded range is essentially an interval, represented by min/max
 * values, along with a flag whether it's a collapsed range (in which case
 * the min and max values are equal). We have the flag to handle by-ref
 * data types - we can't simply compare the datums, and this saves some
 * calls to the type-specific comparator function.
 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:239

/* lower boundary */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:240

/* 上限 */

/* upper boundary */

Japanese translation

/* minval==maxval の場合に true */

/* true if minval==maxval */

Japanese translation

 * 2つの範囲間の距離を表します（拡張範囲の配列へのインデックスで識別されます）。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Represents a distance between two ranges (identified by index into
 * an array of extended ranges).
 */

Japanese translation

 * cmp関数（BTLessStrategyNumberである必要がある）を使用して、
 * 配列の値の順序が正しいことを確認する。
 */

/*
 * Check that the order of the array values is correct, using the cmp
 * function (which should be BTLessStrategyNumber).
 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:292-294

/*
 * Ranges 構造体の包括的なチェック。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Comprehensive check of the Ranges structure.
 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:301

/* 基本的なサニティチェック */

Original comment

/* some basic sanity checks */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:308-313

/*
	 * まず範囲について - 2*nranges 個の境界値があり、値は厳密に順序付けられている必要があります（値が等しい場合は範囲が縮小していることを意味し、点として格納されるべきです）。これにより、範囲が重複しないことも保証されます。
	 */

Original comment

/*
	 * First the ranges - there are 2*nranges boundary values, and the values
	 * have to be strictly ordered (equal values would mean the range is
	 * collapsed, and should be stored as a point). This also guarantees that
	 * the ranges do not overlap.
	 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:316

/* 次に単一点範囲（nvalues 個の境界値を持つ） */

Original comment

/* then the single-point ranges (with nvalues boundary values ) */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:320-323

/*
	 * 値がいずれの範囲にも含まれていないものがないか確認する（ソート済み、未ソートの両方）
	 */

Original comment

/*
	 * Check that none of the values are not covered by ranges (both sorted
	 * and unsorted)
	 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:337-340

/*
			 * 値が最初の範囲の下限よりも小さい場合、
			 * どの範囲にも含まれることはあり得ない。
			 */

Original comment

/*
			 * If the value is smaller than the lower bound in the first range
			 * then it cannot possibly be in any of the ranges.
			 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:346-350

/*
			 * 同様に、値が最終的な範囲の上限よりも大きい場合、
			 * その値がいずれの範囲内にも含まれることはあり得ません。
			 */

Original comment

/*
			 * Likewise, if the value is larger than the upper bound of the
			 * final range, then it cannot possibly be inside any of the
			 * ranges.
			 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:354

/* 'value' がいずれかの範囲内に収まるかどうかを bsearch で検索する */

Original comment

/* bsearch the ranges to see if 'value' fits within any of them */

/* 最初の範囲 */

/* first range */

/* last range */

Japanese translation

/* 範囲から最小値/最大値をコピーする */

Original comment

/* copy the min/max values from the ranges */

/*
				 * 値は minval より小さいか？もしそうなら、範囲配列の左側に再帰する。
				 */

/*
				 * Is the value smaller than the minval? If yes, we'll recurse
				 * to the left side of range array.

Japanese translation

/* この範囲内の最小値よりも小さい */

/* smaller than the smallest value in this range */

				 */

/*
				 * Is the value greater than the minval? If yes, we'll recurse
				 * to the right side of range array.
				 */

Japanese translation

/* この範囲内の最大値よりも大きい */

Original comment

/* larger than the largest value in this range */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:1014

/* hey, 一致する範囲が見つかりました */

Original comment

/* hey, we found a matching range */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:401

/* また、ソートされていない部分の値は、ソート済みの部分に含まれていてはならない */

Original comment

/* and values in the unsorted part must not be in the sorted part */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:421-424

/*
 * 展開された範囲（範囲をいくつか結合して数を減らす際に構築されるもの）が
 * 正しくソートされており、重複していないことを確認する。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Check that the expanded ranges (built when reducing the number of ranges
 * by combining some of them) are correctly sorted and do not overlap.
 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:441-444

/*
	 * 各範囲は独立して有効である必要がある。つまり、境界値において (lower <= upper) であること。
	 */

Original comment

/*
	 * Each range independently should be valid, i.e. that for the boundary
	 * values (lower <= upper).
	 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:451

/* 折りたたみ時: minval == maxval */

Original comment

/* collapsed: minval == maxval */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:453

/* 非折りたたみ時: minval < maxval */

Original comment

/* non-collapsed: minval < maxval */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:459-462

/*
	 * また、範囲は順序付けられている必要があり、重複してはなりません。つまり、連続する範囲の境界において、upper < lower である必要があります。
	 */

Original comment

/*
	 * And the ranges should be ordered and must not overlap, i.e. upper <
	 * lower for boundaries of consecutive ranges.
	 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:477-484

/*
 * minmax_multi_init
 * 		デシリアライズされた範囲リストを初期化し、すべてのメモリを割り当てる。
 *
 * これは範囲のメモリ内表現に過ぎないため、最大値の数に十分な領域を割り当てる
 * （範囲の拡大に伴う repalloc を避けるため）。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * minmax_multi_init
 * 		Initialize the deserialized range list, allocate all the memory.
 *
 * This is only in-memory representation of the ranges, so we allocate
 * enough space for the maximum number of values (so as not to have to do
 * repallocs as the ranges grow).
 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:493

/* 固定ヘッダー */

Original comment

/* fixed header */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:494

/* 日付値 */

Original comment

/* Datum values */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:504-514

/*
 * range_deduplicate_values
 *		単純なポイント内の値を持つ部分を重複排除する。
 *
 * これは、範囲のサイズを削減するためのより低コストな方法を意図している。
 * 範囲には一切触れず、他の値をソートするだけである。距離関数のような
 * 非常にコストのかかる可能性のある処理は呼び出さない。
 *
 * 新しい値を追加する前にチェックを行っているため、値が範囲と重複していないことは
 * 保証されている。ソート済みの値の部分についても同様である。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * range_deduplicate_values
 *		Deduplicate the part with values in the simple points.
 *
 * This is meant to be a cheaper way of reducing the size of the ranges. It
 * does not touch the ranges, and only sorts the other values - it does not
 * call the distance functions, which may be quite expensive, etc.
 *
 * We do know the values are not duplicate with the ranges, because we check
 * that before adding a new value. Same for the sorted part of values.
 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:523-526

/*
	 * 未ソートの値がなければ終了（未ソート部分に値を追加しているため、
	 * このケースは発生しないはずである）。
	 */

Original comment

/*
	 * If there are no unsorted values, we're done (this probably can't
	 * happen, as we're adding values to unsorted part).
	 */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:1501

/* 値をソートする */

Original comment

/* sort the values */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:534

/* 値は範囲（常にソート済み）の直後から始まります */

Original comment

/* the values start right after the ranges (which are always sorted) */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:537-541

/*
	 * XXX 配列の最初の部分が既にソートされていることを利用して、マージソートを行う可能性がある。
	 * ソート済みの部分が大きい場合、かなり高速になる可能性がある。
	 */

Original comment

/*
	 * XXX This might do a merge sort, to leverage that the first part of the
	 * array is already sorted. If the sorted part is large, it might be quite
	 * a bit faster.
	 */

Japanese translation

 * ヘッダーをコピーし、続いてメモリ上の値配列に格納されている個々の値をコピーする。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * brin_range_serialize
 *	  Serialize the in-memory representation into a compact varlena value.
 *
 * Simply copy the header and then also the individual values, as stored
 * in the in-memory value array.
 */

/* ヘッダーは常に必要です */

Original comment

/* header is always needed */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:615-620

/*
	 * 必要なスペースはデータ型に依存します。固定長データ型
	 * （値渡しおよび一部の参照渡し）の場合は非常に単純で、単に
	 * (attlen * nvalues) を乗算するだけで完了します。可変長の参照渡し
	 * 型の場合は、実際にすべての値を走査して長さを合計する必要があります。
	 */

Original comment

/*
	 * The space needed depends on data type - for fixed-length data types
	 * (by-value and some by-reference) it's pretty simple, just multiply
	 * (attlen * nvalues) and we're done. For variable-length by-reference
	 * types we need to actually walk all the values and sum the lengths.
	 */

Japanese translation

/* varlena */

Original comment

/* varlena */

Japanese translation

src/backend/access/brin/brin_minmax_multi.c:826

/* cstring */

Japanese translation
